facts.text: North Gates CDP sits at the geographic center of Monroe County, a spot marked just southwest of the town hall.
The town’s name honors Revolutionary War General Horatio Gates, who inspired the 1813 incorporation of the area formerly called Northampton.
The Hinchey Homestead, a clapboard farmhouse built by early settler William Hinchey, now serves as a museum managed by the Gates Historical Society.
The Gates Historical Society, founded in 1999, preserves local history and showcases the Hinchey Homestead as a model historic site.
The town hall, community center, police department, library, and court offices all share a single building on Buffalo Road, opened in 1969.
The local library offers genealogy resources, including Ancestry.com access, and houses a collection of historic maps and photographs of Gates and Rochester.
